analogy: Think of "Secondary Activities" through the chapter's own opening images: Unit-III Chapter-6 Tertiary and Quaternary Activities When you fall ill you go to your family doctor or you call a doctor. Sometimes your parents take you to a hospital for treatment. While in school, you are taught by your teachers. In the event of any... The easy idea is to follow the chapter's own clues one by one instead of memorising a loose definition. First notice the words and examples the text repeats: TYPES OF TERTIARY ACTIVITIES, Secondary Activities, Secondary, Activities. Then connect each example to the question the chapter is trying to answer. Like solving a source-backed puzzle, every useful point must come from the chapter text itself and stay traceable to the NCERT source.
full_notes:
Secondary Activities is decoded here only from the NCERT chapter text. The chapter begins with its own framing, examples and questions, then builds the topic through source-backed facts. The notes below keep that order: first the opening problem, then the important terms, then the mechanisms, examples and exam-useful connections that appear in the chapter itself.

The text explains that unit-III Chapter-6 Tertiary and Quaternary Activities When you fall ill you go to your family doctor or you call a doctor.

The text explains that sometimes your parents take you to a hospital for treatment.

The text explains that while in school, you are taught by your teachers.

The text explains that in the event of any dispute, legal opinion is obtained from a lawyer.

The text explains that likewise, there are many professionals who provide their services against payment of their fee.

The text explains that thus, all types of services are special skills provided in exchange of payments.

The text explains that health, education, law, governance and recreation etc. require professional skills.

The text explains that these services require other theoretical knowledge and practical training.

The text explains that tertiary activities are related to the service sector.

The text explains that manpower is an important component of the service sector as most of the tertiary activities are performed by skilled labour, professionally trained experts and consultants.

The text explains that in the initial stages of economic development, larger proportion of people worked in the primary sector.

The text explains that in a developed economy, the majority of workers get employment in tertiary activity and a moderate proportion is employed in the secondary sector.

The text explains that tertiary activities include both production and exchange.

The text explains that the production involves the ‘provision’ of services that are ‘consumed’.

The text explains that the output is indirectly measured in terms of wages and salaries.

The text explains that exchange, involves trade, transport and communication facilities that are used to overcome distance.

The text explains that they are not directly involved in the processing of physical raw materials.

The text explains that the main difference between secondary activities and tertiary activities is that the expertise provided by services relies more heavily on specialised skills, experience and knowledge of the workers rather than on the production techniques, machinery and factory processes.

The text explains that retail Trading This is the business activity concerned with the sale of goods directly to the consumers.

The text explains that chain stores are able to purchase merchandise most economically, often going so far as to direct the goods to be manufactured to their specification.

The text explains that some large stores including chain stores are able to buy directly from the manufacturers.

The text explains that however, most retail stores procure supplies from an intermediary source.

The text explains that modern society requires speedy and efficient transport systems to assist in the production, distribution and consumption of goods.

The text explains that at every stage in this complex system, the value of the material is significantly enhanced by transportation.

The text explains that network and Accessibility As transport systems develop, different places are linked together to form a network.

The text explains that all forms are not fully disassociated because of the cheapness of the older systems.

The text explains that besides, the recent advancements like mobile telephony have made communications direct and instantaneous at any time and from anywhere.

The text explains that satellite communication relays information of the earth and from space.

The text explains that the internet has truly revolutionised the global communication system .

The text explains that some are geared to industry, some to people, and some to both industry and people, e.g. the transport systems.

The text explains that making and maintaining highways and bridges, maintaining fire fighting departments and supplying or supervising education and customer -care are among the important services most often supervised or performed by governments or companies.

The text explains that tourism fosters the growth of infrastructure industries, retail trading, and craft industries (souvenirs).

The text explains that this is one of the main reasons for the importance of tourism in Southern Europe and the Mediterranean lands.

The text explains that the Mediterranean climate offers almost consistently higher temperatures, than in other parts of Europe, long hours of sunshine and low rainfall throughout the peak holiday season.

The text explains that people taking winter holidays have specific climatic requirements, either higher temperatures than their own homelands, or snow cover suitable for skiing.

The text explains that history and Art: The history and art of an area have potential attractiveness.

The text explains that culture and Economy: These attract tourists with a penchant for experiencing ethnic and local customs.

The text explains that this is still a small number compared with the millions of surgeries performed each year in the U.S. healthcare system.

The text explains that like some of the tertiary functions, quaternary activities can also be outsourced.

The text explains that they are not tied to resources, affected by the environment, or necessarily localised by market.

The text explains that often referred to as ‘gold collar’ professions, they represent another subdivision of the tertiary sector representing special and highly paid skills of senior business executives, government officials, research scientists, financial and legal consultants, etc.

The text explains that their importance in the structure of advanced economies far outweighs their numbers.

The text explains that the comparative advantage is the main reason for continuing outsourcing.

The text explains that new trends in quinary services include knowledge processing outsourcing (KPO) and ‘home shoring’, the latter as an alternative to outsourcing.

The text explains that the KPO industry is distinct from Business Process Outsourcing (BPO) as it involves highly skilled workers.

The text explains that business activities that are outsourced include information technology (IT), human resources, customer support and call centre services and at times also manufacturing and engineering.

The text explains that data processing is an IT related service easily be carried out in Asian, East European and African countries, In these countries IT skilled staff with good English language skills are available at lower wages than those in the developed countries.

The text explains that choose the right answer from the four alternatives given below.

The text explains that (b) At times engineering and manufacturing jobs can also be outsourced.

The text explains that (a) Primary (c) Secondary (b) Quaternary (d) Service (iv) Jobs that involve high degrees and level of innovations are known as: (a) Secondary activities (c) Quinary activities (b) Quaternary activities (d) Primary activities (v) Which one of the following activities is related to quaternary sector?

The text explains that (a) Manufacturing computers (c) University teaching (b) Paper and Raw pulp production (d) Printing books (vi) Which one out of the following statements is not true?

The text explains that (a) Outsourcing reduces costs and increases efficiency.

Source: NCERT Class 12 Fundamentals of Human Geography, Chapter 6: Secondary Activities

Do not add outside examples to Secondary Activities; use only the examples and processes named in the chapter.
Do not confuse a visible surface result with the internal process or source evidence described by the text.
